commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds startup plugin-id scoping for metadata snapshot manifest reconstruction, carries `activation.onConfigPaths` into installed-index startup metadata, and adds focused plugin startup tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the PR writes and consumes `startup.configPaths`, while the persisted installed-index Zod schema still omits that field, so a persisted read can drop the metadata required for scoped startup.
commands.review_followup: A narrow source-backed repair remains: keep the new `startup.configPaths` field through persisted index parsing and add roundtrip coverage.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(plugins): scope startup metadata manifest reads
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway startup could still rebuild plugin metadata from every installed plugin manifest before narrowing to the configured startup set.
- Solution: carry a conservative startup plugin-id scope into metadata snapshot loading when the Installed Plugin Index can prove the narrowed set safely.
- What changed: metadata snapshots can carry scoped `pluginIds`; lookup/startup paths pass a conservative `pluginIdScope`; startup scoping carries indexed `activation.onConfigPaths` owners such as `browser`; scoped snapshots are not reused for configless default discovery.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no plugin config shape changes, no provider/channel activation semantics changes, no compat bundled-discovery shortcut.

## Motivation

#70533 is a startup stability problem: a restrictive plugin setup should not crash-loop or pay full manifest reconstruction cost just because unrelated installed plugins exist. This keeps the startup path on the Installed Plugin Index when it can safely prove the manifest subset, and falls back to the old unscoped behavior when it cannot.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #70533
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: restrictive gateway startup plugin scope still caused full installed-index manifest reconstruction before startup narrowing.
- Real environment tested: isolated Linux testserver disposable repo, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.2, `origin/main` at `a13468320c` compared with this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran TSX diagnostic scripts through the real `loadPluginLookUpTable -> loadPluginMetadataSnapshot -> loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex -> resolveGatewayStartupPluginPlanFromRegistry` path using valid 96-plugin Installed Plugin Index fixtures.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "label": "after-rebased-patch-confirm",
  "indexPluginCount": 96,
  "manifestPluginCount": 1,
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "loadedPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "diagnosticCount": 0
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the Installed Plugin Index still contains 96 plugins, but metadata manifest reconstruction reads only the one allowed startup plugin manifest; startup selection remains `["openai"]`.
- Additional blocker-fix proof:

```json
{
  "config": {
    "browser": { "enabled": true },
    "channels": {},
    "plugins": {
      "allow": ["openai"],
      "slots": { "memory": "none" }
    }
  },
  "results": [
    {
      "label": "indexed-config-path-scope",
      "indexPluginCount": 96,
      "manifestPluginCount": 2,
      "startupPluginIds": ["openai", "browser"],
      "loadedPluginIds": ["browser", "openai"],
      "diagnosticCount": 0
    },
    {
      "label": "legacy-index-safe-fallback",
      "indexPluginCount": 96,
      "manifestPluginCount": 96,
      "startupPluginIds": ["openai", "browser"],
      "diagnosticCount": 0
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed blocker-fix result: `browser.enabled=true` is preserved under a restrictive `plugins.allow=["openai"]`; new indexes stay scoped to 2 manifests, while legacy indexes without indexed config-path metadata fall back unscoped instead of losing `browser`.
- What was not tested: no production credentials, no persistent services, no real external provider/channel account.
- Before evidence:

```json
{
  "label": "before-rebased-origin-main",
  "indexPluginCount": 96,
  "manifestPluginCount": 96,
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "diagnosticCount": 0
}
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: metadata snapshot loading had no way to receive a conservative startup plugin-id scope after the Installed Plugin Index was available, so it reconstructed the installed manifest registry for the full index before startup planning narrowed plugin ids.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered startup narrowing and metadata memoization separately, but not the manifest reconstruction count for restrictive gateway startup allowlists.
- Contributing context (if known): some startup intent, config-path activation, channel, provider, slot, and harness decisions can only be trusted when the Installed Plugin Index can map them without reading manifests; unsafe cases need to stay unscoped.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/plugin-lookup-table.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: restrictive `plugins.allow` startup config passes a narrow scope to installed-index manifest reconstruction; config-path activation owners such as `browser` stay included; conservative fallback remains unscoped for compat discovery, legacy indexes, or unmappable providers.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it asserts the actual manifest-registry load boundary without needing external plugin credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Gateway startup with a restrictive plugin allowlist can avoid reading unrelated installed plugin manifests when the startup scope is safe to prove from the Installed Plugin Index. Unsafe configs keep existing unscoped behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Installed Index (96 plugins) -> read 96 manifests -> startup plan -> openai

After:
Installed Index (96 plugins) -> conservative scope ["browser","openai"] when browser config is present -> read 2 manifests -> startup plan -> openai + browser
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux testserver disposable run
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): synthetic installed plugin fixture; no external channel account
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.allow=["openai"]`, `plugins.slots.memory="none"`; blocker regression also uses `browser.enabled=true`

### Steps

1. On `origin/main`, run the diagnostic fixture through `loadPluginLookUpTable`.
2. Apply this patch.
3. Run the same diagnostic fixture again.
4. Run focused regression tests, format, typecheck, and targeted lint.

### Expected

- Startup still selects `openai`.
- `browser.enabled=true` still selects `browser` even when `plugins.allow` only lists `openai`.
- Manifest reconstruction only reads the scoped startup plugin(s) after the patch.
- Conservative fallback paths remain unscoped when the Installed Plugin Index cannot prove the mapping.

### Actual

- Before: `manifestPluginCount=96`, `startupPluginIds=["openai"]`.
- After: `manifestPluginCount=1`, `loadedPluginIds=["openai"]`, `startupPluginIds=["openai"]`.
- After blocker repair: `manifestPluginCount=2`, `loadedPluginIds=["browser","openai"]`, `startupPluginIds=["openai","browser"]` for `browser.enabled=true`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [x] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-lookup-table.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry-installed.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-contract-eligibility.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
10 files / 269 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-lookup-table.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry-installed.ts src/plugins/effective-plugin-ids.ts src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts
passed

git diff --check
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json <11 changed files>
0 warnings / 0 errors

pnpm check:changed
attempted; conflict/changelog/dependency guards plus core and core-test TypeScript passed, then full core lint was killed by `tsgolint` SIGKILL on the testserver. Targeted Oxlint for the changed repair files passed with 0 warnings / 0 errors.
```

Additional note: `origin/main @ a13468320c` failed `test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json` before this patch because `status.summary.redaction.test.ts` had not been updated for the newer `SessionStatus` fields. This PR includes that test-only fixture update so `check:changed` can pass cleanly on the rebased branch.

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: before/after runtime manifest reconstruction count on the real OpenClaw metadata lookup path; restrictive allowlist scope; config-path activation owner retention; legacy index safe fallback; compat discovery fallback; unmappable provider fallback; scoped/unscoped memo separation; configless scoped snapshot reuse rejection.
- Edge cases checked: disabled plugin entries are removed from the scope, direct configured channels/providers only scope when the Installed Plugin Index can map them, scope ids outside the index and legacy config-path activation metadata fall back to unscoped loading.
- What I did **not** verify: real production provider/channel credentials or long-running gateway service startup.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: overly aggressive startup scoping could hide a plugin whose startup intent is only manifest-visible.
  - Mitigation: scope is only used when allowlists/config entries/config-path activation/slots/direct channel/provider/harness ids can be mapped from the Installed Plugin Index; ambiguous compat, provider, channel, legacy-index, or out-of-index cases return to unscoped manifest reconstruction.
target.author: iwhatsskill

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed
